title: Pseuderanthemum repandum
taxonomy: Pseuderanthemum repandum is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is a member of the Acanthaceae family, which consists of various species of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thistle family. Acanthaceae is a large family of plants, with over 2,000 species across the world. Pseuderanthemum repandum is further classified under the genus Pseuderanthemum, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Pseuderanthemum repandum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Lamiales, Family: Acanthaceae, Genus: Pseuderanthemum, Species: P. repandum.

intro: Pseuderanthemum repandum, also known as 'False Eranthemum', is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseuderanthemum repandum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
